answer:
**Claude Cowork** is an autonomous desktop agent designed for non-technical knowledge workers, launched in research preview on January 12, 2026, and reaching General Availability (GA) across all paid plans (Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise) on April 9, 2026 [1-3]. Developed internally by Boris Cherny in just ten days using Claude Code, Cowork brings agentic, multi-step execution to the desktop environment, allowing users to define a goal and step away while the AI completes the work [4-6]. 

### How Claude Cowork Works
Unlike standard conversational AI, Cowork operates on a continuous **"plan-to-action" execution loop** [7]. 
*   **Virtual Machine Isolation:** Once you grant it access to specific local folders, Cowork runs shell commands and code within a secure, sandboxed Linux Virtual Machine (VM) running locally on your computer (utilizing Apple's `VZVirtualMachine` framework on macOS) [8-10]. 
*   **Task Execution:** When you assign a goal, Cowork analyzes the workspace, drafts a structured plan, breaks the request into subtasks, and executes them by reading, creating, and editing files directly on your file system [7, 10, 11]. It can even coordinate multiple sub-agents to work on different parts of a problem in parallel [7, 11].
*   **Computer Use:** Cowork can simulate screen interactions to open local files, navigate custom SaaS dashboards, click buttons, and fill out forms on your active host screen [12]. 
*   **Security Controls:** You can operate Cowork in **"Ask before acting"** mode (pausing for manual approval before running scripts) or **"Act without asking"** mode (continuous background execution) [13, 14]. Regardless of the mode, the agent faces an un-bypassable desktop prompt requiring your explicit permission before permanently deleting any files [14, 15]. 

### Best Use Cases & Tactics
Cowork excels at high-effort, repeatable operational labor [16, 17]. 
*   **File and Data Automation:** You can ask Cowork to organize a messy Downloads folder by sorting hundreds of files into categorized directories, applying batch renames (e.g., `YYYY-MM-DD_filename`), detecting outliers in datasets, or turning folders of unstructured receipts into formatted expense reports [8, 12, 18]. 
*   **Polished Deliverables:** Rather than generating raw CSV text, Cowork can programmatically build formatted Microsoft Excel models with functional formulas, or compile competitor research into a PowerPoint deck that respects your brand fonts, slide masters, and color schemes [12, 19]. 
*   **Scheduled Tasks:** Using the `/schedule` command, you can set recurring automations. For example, you can tell Claude once to compile a weekly data report or check competitor pricing every Monday, and it will run hands-free [12, 20].
*   **Mobile Task "Dispatching":** As of March 2026, the **Dispatch** feature allows you to text a command from the Claude mobile app while away from your desk. Your phone acts as a remote control, prompting your desktop machine to spin up Cowork, execute the task locally, and send a push notification to your phone when the final deliverable is ready [21-24].
*   **Small Business Workflows:** Through the May 2026 "Claude for Small Business" expansion, Cowork natively connects to tools like Intuit QuickBooks, PayPal, Canva, and HubSpot. You can ask Cowork to automatically reconcile ledger accounts, draft promotional strategies, generate Canva assets, and prepare a month-end close packet [25]. 

### Current Limits and Security Risks
*   **Infrastructure Dependencies:** Because Cowork runs on your local machine, your desktop computer must remain awake, connected to the internet, and have the Claude Desktop app open. If your computer goes to sleep, active tasks and scheduled jobs will pause [24, 26, 27].
*   **Token Burn & Cost:** Agentic multi-step tasks are highly compute-intensive. Heavy use of Cowork will drain your usage limits significantly faster than standard chatting [28, 29].
*   **Enterprise Integration Gaps:** While you can stream Cowork events via OpenTelemetry for SIEM monitoring, Cowork activity is **not yet captured in the Claude Compliance API**, limiting its auditability for strictly regulated enterprises [30, 31]. Furthermore, memory is not retained across standalone Cowork sessions unless you operate inside a specific Project [26].
*   **Prompt Injection Risks:** Using "Act without asking" mode paired with internet access or the Claude in Chrome extension carries significant risk. If Cowork encounters a malicious webpage or a compromised local document, hidden instructions could hijack the agent to execute unintended actions before you can intervene [32-34].

### How it Differs from Claude.ai Chat and Claude Code
Anthropic describes these tools as "one engine, three jobs"—they all use the same underlying models (like Sonnet 4.6 or Opus 4.8), but provide different hands for different audiences [35-37].

1.  **Claude (The Chat App):** Built for **thinking and drafting**. It requires a human in the loop for every turn. You guide it step-by-step, and it cannot directly reach into your file system or execute local scripts. It is entirely conversational [38-40].
2.  **Claude Code:** Built for **software engineers**. It lives entirely in the terminal or IDE (VS Code/JetBrains). It autonomously navigates codebases, writes tests, runs compilation commands, fixes syntax errors, and manages Git branches and pull requests [41-43]. 
3.  **Claude Cowork:** Built for **non-technical knowledge workers**. It replaces the command line with a graphical desktop interface. While Claude Code outputs committed software, Cowork outputs operational business deliverables (spreadsheets, presentations, cleaned directories) without the user ever touching a terminal [44-46].

The Autonomous Desktop Agent Revolution: A Strategic Assessment of Claude Cowork in 2026
Claude Cowork | Anthropic's agentic AI for knowledge work \ Anthropic
What is Claude Cowork?

The program was developed by Boris Cherny over a ten-day period using Claude Code, demonstrating the recursive capabilities of agentic frameworks.[1, 5] While Claude Code remains optimized for software developers running terminal-based commands, Claude Cowork is designed for non-technical business professionals who require multi-step file manipulation, application orchestration, and document compilation without terminal interaction.[2, 4, 6]

In March 2026, the introduction of the Dispatch framework established a bidirectional bridge between mobile devices and local desktop environments.[5, 10] This framework unifies mobile and desktop sessions, allowing users to delegate tasks from the Claude mobile application on iOS or Android and have them processed by the active desktop computer.[10]
—Daniela Amodei, Co-founder and President of Anthropic

Cowork The desktop agent for everyone else. For documents, data, files, and business workflows, no terminal required.

What changes is the hands. Claude the app responds to you turn by turn and produces text you act on. Claude Code and Cowork can read your files, run steps, and return a finished result. And among those two agents, the split is about audience: Code lives in the terminal and the IDE for people who write software, while Cowork lives in the desktop app for people who do knowledge work but do not want a command line.
Underpinning Models, Compute Logistics, and Financial Structures
At a glance
Claude: the app for thinking
A standard corporate initiative can be divided into three phases: scoping and conceptualizing (Think), building and implementing (Build), and automating daily operations (Operate).[6]
Use Claude when

You set a goal, it plans and executes, you approve changes
